data.title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Double-Blind, Parallel-Group, Placebo-Controlled, Duloxetine-Referenced, Fixed-Dose Study Comparing the Efficacy and Safety of 2 Doses (15 and 20 mg) of Lu AA21004 in Acute Treatment of Adults With Major Depressive Disorder
data.brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to evaluate the efficacy, safety and tolerability of vortioxetine, once daily (QD), compared with placebo in adults with major depressive disorder.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

1. Man or a woman who suffers from a major depressive episode (MDE) recurrent as the primary diagnosis according to the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, Fourth Edition, Text Revision (DSM-IV-TR) criteria (classification code 296.3x) as confirmed by the Stru
endpoints: Change From Baseline in the Montgomery-Åsberg Depression Rating Scale (MADRS) Total Score

data.title: A Randomized, Double-blind, Parallel-Group, Placebo-Controlled, Fixed-Dose Study Comparing the Efficacy and Safety of 3 Doses of Lu AA21004 in Acute Treatment of Adults With Major Depressive Disorder
data.country: Australia, United Kingdom, France, South Korea, Netherlands, Germany, Latvia, Malaysia, Czechia, Russia, Ukraine, South Africa, Taiwan, Lithuania, Poland
data.brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to determine the effectiveness and safety of vortioxetine, once daily (QD), in patients with Major Depressive Disorder.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Has a primary diagnosis of major depressive episode according to the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, Fourth Edition, Text Revision (DSM-IV-TR) criteria.
* The reported duration of the current major depressive episode is at least 3 months.
* Has a Montgom
endpoints: Change From Baseline in the 24-item Hamilton Depression Scale Total Score At Week 8

data.title: A Phase 3, Long-Term, Open-Label, Flexible-Dose, Extension Study Evaluating the Safety and Tolerability of Lu AA21004 (15 and 20 mg) in Subjects With Major Depressive Disorder
data.brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to determine the long-term safety and tolerability of vortioxetine, once daily (QD), in participants with major depressive disorder.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Has completed either study LuAA21004\_315 ( NCT01153009), LuAA21004\_316 (NCT01163266), or LuAA21004\_317 (NCT01179516) immediately prior to enrollment in the extension study (ie, the baseline visit is the same visit as the Week 8 \[Lu AA21004\_317\] or Week 10 \[Lu AA21004\_3
endpoints: Number of Participants With Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events at a Frequency Threshold of ≥5%
Number of Participants With Serious Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events
Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events Leading to Study Discontinuation

data.title: A Long-Term, Open-Label, Flexible-Dose, Extension Study Evaluating the Safety and Tolerability of Lu AA21004 in Subjects With Major Depressive Disorder
data.country: Australia, United Kingdom, France, South Korea, Croatia, Netherlands, Germany, Latvia, United States, Malaysia, Russia, Ukraine, South Africa, Taiwan, Serbia, Lithuania, Poland
data.brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to determine the long-term efficacy and safety of vortioxetine, once daily (QD), in adults with major depressive disorder.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Has completed the double blind treatment period of either study Lu AA21004\_304 (NCT00672620) or LuAA21004\_305 (NCT00735709) immediately prior to enrollment in the extension study (ie, the baseline visit is the same visit as the completion visit of the double blind treatment
endpoints: Number of Participants With Potentially Clinically Significant Laboratory Evaluation Findings
Number of Participants With Potentially Clinically Significant Electrocardiogram (ECG) Findings

data.title: A Multinational, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Dose Ranging Study to Assess the Efficacy and Safety of Lu AA21004 in Patients With Major Depressive Disorder

Inclusion Criteria:

endpoints: Change From Baseline in Montgomery-Åsberg Depression Rating Scale (MADRS) Total Score
